propositions.content: LLM advice output is sensitive to prompt phrasing — the same underlying question worded differently can elicit meaningfully different, sometimes contradictory recommendations.
propositions.note: Prompt sensitivity — observable fact about LLM behaviour

propositions.content: LLMs optimise for coherent, contextually plausible text rather than correctness of judgment; this makes them suboptimal for tasks requiring evaluation of competing options or context-weighted trade-offs.
propositions.note: LLMs not optimal for judgment-intensive tasks

propositions.content: The path to trustworthy AI advice is not reliable output (consistent across all prompt phrasings) but governed output — advice that is bounded, traceable, and repairable when contradictions emerge.
propositions.note: FIRST canonical-claim: governance over reliability for AI advice — exercises new singular category

propositions.content: The path to trustworthy AI advice is reliable output — AI systems that produce consistent recommendations regardless of how the question is phrased, eliminating the need for post-hoc correction.
propositions.note: Opposing canonical-claim: reliability framing — should CONFLICT with R3 (singular enforcement test)

results.note: Prompt sensitivity — observable fact about LLM behaviour
